title: Vulpia elliotea
taxonomy: Vulpia elliotea belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. Within this family, it is classified under the subfamily Pooideae. At the genus level, it is placed in the genus Vulpia, which is a group of annual grasses. The species is further divided into the species Vulpia elliotea, which is native to North America. It is closely related to other species within the Vulpia genus, such as Vulpia bromoides and Vulpia fasciculata.

intro: Vulpia elliotea, also known as Hare's Ear, is a flowering plant species in the grass family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Vulpia elliotea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits.
